title: Intelligence Briefing Dashboard
subtitle: Weekly Conflict Monitor · All Regions
sources: Reuters · AP News · Al Jazeera · ACLED · US Embassy Alerts · LiveUAMap · BNO News · ISW · British Defence Intelligence

regions.name: Middle East
overview: US-Iran direct exchange (10–11 Jun): IRGC drones down US Army Apache near Strait of Hormuz; CENTCOM strikes Iranian radar/air-defence sites; IRGC retaliates with strikes on US military base in Jordan and 21 Gulf targets — most direct US-Iran kinetic exchange since January 2020.

Gaza: IDF maintains 40 permanent military outposts inside Gaza confirmed by satellite; 3,201 ceasefire violations and 72,991 deaths since October 2023; phase-two talks deadlocked on disarmament and governance terms.
Houthi Red Sea resumption (8 Jun): MARAD advisory 2026-006 active; Israeli-linked shipping banned; Saudi Arabia maintaining 70%+ crude export diversion via Yanbu; Cape of Good Hope rerouting resumes.

Lebanon: April 16 ceasefire repeatedly violated; Lebanese army warns it may freeze ceasefire committee cooperation; Hezbollah maintains offensive posture; June 7–8 Iran-Israel exchange worst since April truce.

countries.name: Gaza
incidents: 13 Jun — Gaza Strip: Al Jazeera satellite analysis confirms 40 IDF permanent military outposts built inside Gaza since October 2025 ceasefire; 3,201 ceasefire violations now confirmed.
12 Jun — Gaza City: Israeli airstrikes kill 9 Palestinians in pre-dawn strikes on four residential apartments; Egyptian mediation continues in Cairo.
9 Jun — Central Gaza: Cumulative death toll reaches 72,991 since October 2023, including 20,179 children; UN calls for immediate cessation of hostilities.
7 Jun — Southern Gaza: Israeli army kills 13 Palestinians in morning strikes; Egypt convenes fresh round of ceasefire-hostage mediation.
countries.coa: Gaza's October 2025 ceasefire is nominal — IDF has built 40 permanent outposts inside the Strip and recorded 3,201 violations. Phase-two negotiations remain deadlocked over Hamas disarmament and IDF withdrawal terms. No political resolution within 90 days. Humanitarian catastrophe deepens as aid access remains contested.

countries.name: Lebanon
incidents: 13 Jun — Southern Lebanon: Lebanese army formally warns Israeli airstrikes may force it to freeze cooperation with the ceasefire monitoring committee.
10 Jun — Beirut: Iran suspends military operations conditionally; Israel strikes southern Lebanon within hours of the announcement, maintaining cycle of escalation.
8 Jun — Tyre (Sur): IDF extends evacuation zone; Hezbollah fires anti-tank missiles at IDF patrol near Maroun al-Ras.
7 Jun — Beirut (Dahiyeh): Israeli airstrikes on southern suburbs trigger Iranian 20+ ballistic missile response; tens of thousands flee.
countries.coa: April 16 ceasefire is being systematically dismantled. Lebanese army threat to freeze ceasefire committee cooperation is a significant escalation indicator. Hezbollah is using the Iran-Israel exchange as cover for rearmament in south Lebanon. Risk of full IDF ground incursion HIGH within 90 days if Lebanese army exits the ceasefire framework.

countries.name: Iran
incidents: 11 Jun — Jordan/Gulf: IRGC retaliates for CENTCOM strikes with attacks on US military base in Jordan and 21 Gulf targets — the most direct US-Iran kinetic exchange since January 2020.
10 Jun — Strait of Hormuz: IRGC drones down US Army Apache helicopter near Hormuz; CENTCOM launches retaliatory strikes on Iranian radar and air-defence installations.
8 Jun — Tehran: Iran announces conditional suspension of operations against Israel, demanding Israeli halt to Lebanon strikes; IDF strikes Lebanon within hours.
7 Jun — Iran/Israel: Iran fires 20+ ballistic missiles at Israel following IDF airstrikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh district; majority intercepted.
countries.coa: Iran is now in direct kinetic exchange with the United States — the IRGC attack on a US base in Jordan marks a major threshold. CENTCOM has signalled intent to set terms for a deal, not restart full war. Iranian leverage includes the Hormuz closure threat, Houthi Red Sea operations, and PMF networks across Iraq and Syria. Nuclear talks are effectively frozen while kinetic phase continues. Risk of further US-Iran escalation remains HIGH within 90 days.

countries.name: Yemen
incidents: 13 Jun — Red Sea: MARAD advisory 2026-006 in force; commercial shipping diversion to Cape of Good Hope accelerating; insurance premiums spiking.
11 Jun — Bab al-Mandab: US Navy intercepts Houthi anti-ship ballistic missile; coalition assets at elevated readiness.
8 Jun — Sana'a: Houthis formally announce resumption of Red Sea ban on Israeli-linked shipping; October 2025 maritime pause officially ends.
7 Jun — Marib: Houthi offensive against Saudi-backed positions resumes as IRGC-Houthi coordination deepens.
countries.coa: Houthi Red Sea operations are fully back in force after a 7-month pause, directly linked to the Iran-Israel-US escalation. Global shipping costs will rise as Cape of Good Hope diversions resume. Saudi Arabia's Yanbu diversion provides temporary oil-export resilience but is not sustainable indefinitely. No Yemeni political resolution within 90 days; Houthi trajectory entirely determined by Iran war dynamics.

countries.name: Israel
incidents: 13 Jun — West Bank (Jenin): IDF counter-terrorism operation in Jenin refugee camp; 3 militants killed, weapons cache seized.
11 Jun — Israel (national): IRGC attack on US base in Jordan elevates Israeli threat environment; IDF on Condition Bravo across northern districts.
10 Jun — Lebanon border: IDF strikes southern Lebanon within hours of Iran's conditional suspension announcement; multi-front posture maintained.
7 Jun — Beirut/Iran: IDF strikes Dahiyeh trigger Iranian ballistic missile barrage; Iron Dome and Arrow-3 intercept majority; no reported Israeli casualties.
countries.coa: Israel is simultaneously managing Gaza, Lebanon, the West Bank, direct Iranian missile threat, and an indirect US-Iran confrontation risk. Netanyahu government continues to prioritise military objectives over diplomatic frameworks. Hostage negotiations are paused. Risk of Lebanon ground escalation remains HIGH if Hezbollah continues operating from south Lebanon.

countries.name: Iraq
incidents: 12 Jun — Baghdad: PMF groups back IRGC retaliatory strikes on US base in Jordan; Iraqi government faces renewed pressure to expel US forces.
11 Jun — Anbar (Ain al-Asad): US and Iraqi forces on elevated joint alert following US-Iran kinetic exchange.
9 Jun — Kirkuk: ISIS-linked cell neutralised; 3 militants killed, IED workshop and weapons cache seized.
8 Jun — Basra: Kata'ib Hezbollah declares combat readiness in solidarity with IRGC following Iran-US exchange.
countries.coa: US-Iran kinetic exchange significantly increases direct confrontation risk in Iraq. PMF groups are at their highest combat readiness since 2020. US force presence in Iraq is at risk if Iran escalates further. ISIS reconstitution in Kirkuk and Anbar is a secondary but growing threat.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
incidents: 13 Jun — Red Sea/Yanbu: Saudi Arabia maintains 70%+ crude export diversion to Yanbu; shipping insurance premiums rising.
11 Jun — Riyadh: Saudi Arabia expresses alarm over direct US-Iran exchange; backchannel communication with Tehran maintained.
9 Jun — Eastern Province: Aramco maintains elevated security status as Houthi Red Sea threat resumes.
8 Jun — Red Sea: Saudi naval assets coordinate with US 5th Fleet on Houthi maritime threat monitoring.
countries.coa: Saudi Arabia faces renewed Houthi maritime threat alongside risk of collateral damage from the US-Iran exchange. MBS is pursuing a dual-track approach: US military cooperation plus Tehran backchannel. Yanbu diversion cannot be sustained indefinitely at full rate. Oil market shock risk if Hormuz is actually closed. Saudi-Iran rapprochement track is effectively suspended.

countries.name: Syria
incidents: 13 Jun — Deir ez-Zor: ISIS attack on security post; 3 killed.
11 Jun — Homs Province: Israeli air force strikes IRGC logistics convoy amid ongoing Iran-US tensions; secondary explosions reported.
9 Jun — Damascus outskirts: Israeli strike on Iranian weapons-storage facility destroys significant cache.
8 Jun — Idlib: HTS-Turkish backed faction clash near Saraqib; 3 fighters killed.
countries.coa: Syria remains the primary Israeli target for disrupting Iranian asset networks. US-Iran exchange will intensify Israeli strikes on IRGC positions in Syria. ISIS reconstitution in eastern Syria is accelerating. No political resolution within 90 days.

countries.name: Egypt
incidents: 13 Jun — Cairo: Egypt hosts new round of Hamas-mediator ceasefire talks; no breakthrough.
11 Jun — Rafah: Egypt opens humanitarian corridor; 30 aid trucks admitted — first significant delivery since June 8 Houthi escalation disrupted Red Sea supply chains.
9 Jun — N. Sinai: Security forces neutralise Ansar Bait al-Maqdis cell; 2 militants killed, weapons seized.
countries.coa: Egypt's mediation role remains central but increasingly constrained as Gaza's humanitarian situation deteriorates and regional escalation broadens. North Sinai counter-terrorism operations are ongoing at low level. US-Iran exchange will not directly impact Egyptian security but increases pressure on Cairo's diplomatic balancing act.

countries.name: Jordan
incidents: 11 Jun — Jordan: IRGC launches strikes on US military base in Jordan as part of 22-target retaliation for CENTCOM airstrikes on Iran; Jordan lodges formal diplomatic protest with Tehran.
11 Jun — Amman: King Abdullah convenes emergency NSC; Jordan and US coordinate response to IRGC attack on Jordanian-hosted US base.
9 Jun — Eastern Border: Jordan intercepts weapons-smuggling attempt from Syria; 6 suspects arrested.
7 Jun — Jordan/Israel: Jordan assists Israeli air-defence tracking as Iranian ballistic missiles transit Jordanian airspace.
countries.coa: Jordan's threat level elevated following the direct IRGC strike on a US base on Jordanian soil — first confirmed Iranian strike inside Jordan in the current conflict cycle. King Abdullah faces acute pressure managing the US-Iran confrontation occurring on Jordanian territory. Risk of further IRGC targeting in Jordan remains elevated through the 90-day window.

countries.name: Libya
incidents: 13 Jun — Sirte: GNU and LNA exchange artillery fire; ceasefire on western front temporarily suspended.
11 Jun — Benghazi: LNA intercepts Turkish-linked weapons shipment; maritime interdiction continues.
9 Jun — Sabha: Arms trafficking network disrupted; 3 suspects with Sahel-bound weapons cache arrested.
7 Jun — Mediterranean: 310+ migrants rescued; 4 drowning deaths; departure rate from Libyan coast remains high.
countries.coa: Libya remains a dual-government failed state. GNU-LNA tensions are periodically kinetic but below full-conflict threshold. Arms and migrant flows to Europe and Sahel continue unabated. No unification pathway visible within 90 days.

countries.name: Tunisia
incidents: 13 Jun — Tunis: Security services detain 5 suspected JNIM-linked facilitators in ongoing counter-terrorism sweep.
11 Jun — Medenine: Border reinforcement following intelligence assessment of Libya-based network activity.
8 Jun — Sfax: Two suspects arrested in migrant departure facilitation network interdiction.
countries.coa: Tunisia faces a low-level jihadist threat from Libya-linked networks. Political repression under President Saied continues with no reform trajectory. No major escalation expected within 90 days.

regions.name: Ukraine/Russia
overview: Russia Day strikes (12 Jun): Ukraine's largest drone campaign of 2026 — Taneko and Taif-NK refineries in Tatarstan plus Togliattikauchuk chemical plant in Samara struck; Russia claims 231 drones intercepted; oil revenue and refinery capacity further degraded.

Pokrovsk (13 Jun): Ukrainian SOF (4th Ranger Regiment and 7th Rapid Response Corps) destroy 6 Russian VTOL drone launch sites concealed in civilian buildings; Russian tactical drone pressure on Pokrovsk axis temporarily reduced.
Sustained deep-strike campaign: St. Petersburg oil terminal struck 3 Jun; Mariupol port and Sevastopol targeted 10 Jun; logistics disruption accelerating across multiple Russian regions.

Frontline stalemate: Russian advance on Pokrovsk axis slowed to a tactical crawl; neither side has operational breakthrough capacity; drone overmatch and logistics interdiction increasingly determine outcomes over massed assault.

countries.name: Ukraine
incidents: 13 Jun — Pokrovsk: Ukrainian SOF destroy 6 Russian VTOL drone launch sites hidden in civilian buildings; VTOL pressure on Pokrovsk axis temporarily reduced.
12 Jun — Multiple Russian regions: Russia Day drone campaign strikes Taneko and Taif-NK refineries in Tatarstan plus Togliattikauchuk chemical plant in Samara; fires confirmed at all three targets.
10 Jun — Mariupol/Crimea: Ukrainian drones strike Mariupol port and Sevastopol museum; Russian nighttime train schedules suspended across Crimea.
7 Jun — Kharkiv Oblast: Russia launches 171-drone overnight barrage against Ukraine; majority intercepted; residential buildings damaged in Kharkiv city.
countries.coa: Ukraine's deep-strike campaign is systematically degrading Russian oil revenue and logistics capacity. Russia Day targeting of Tatarstan/Samara refineries represents the deepest economic disruption of the war. Pokrovsk axis remains under Russian pressure but advance rate has slowed markedly. No major Ukrainian offensive expected within 90 days — attrition through long-range strike remains the strategy. Western arms continuity is critical.

countries.name: Russia
incidents: 12 Jun — Tatarstan/Samara: Ukraine's Russia Day drone campaign strikes Taneko and Taif-NK oil refineries plus Togliattikauchuk chemical plant; Russia claims 231 drones intercepted; fires at all three targets.
12 Jun — Crimea: Sevastopol targeted; historic museum struck by Ukrainian drones; nighttime rail schedules restricted island-wide.
10 Jun — Mariupol (occupied): Ukrainian drone strike on Mariupol port disrupts logistics operations.
3 Jun — St. Petersburg: Ukrainian drones strike oil terminal ahead of Putin visit; fire contained; significant strategic disruption.
countries.coa: Ukraine's escalating deep-strike campaign is imposing real economic costs — oil refinery capacity and petrochemical output are being systematically degraded. Russia will maintain attritional strategy on Pokrovsk axis while sustaining nightly drone barrages against Ukrainian infrastructure. Tactical drone overmatch has shifted to Ukraine. No major Russian operational breakthrough expected in 90-day window.

countries.name: Belarus
incidents: 13 Jun — Gomel Oblast: Russian military logistics convoy observed transiting south; Belarusian rail corridor active for war supplies.
10 Jun — Poland/Belarus border: Hybrid pressure campaign continues; 290 crossing attempts in 48 hours; Frontex on elevated status.
8 Jun — Minsk: Lukashenko hosts Russian military delegation; logistics and air-defence integration coordination.
countries.coa: Belarus remains a primary Russian logistics enabler. Lukashenko will not commit Belarusian forces directly to the war. Hybrid pressure on Poland and Baltic borders will continue as a low-cost destabilisation tool.

countries.name: Moldova
incidents: 12 Jun — Chisinau: Moldova requests additional EU border monitoring capacity amid elevated Transnistria movement reports.
9 Jun — Transnistria: Pro-Russian separatists hold demonstration; Russian military equipment sightings near Tiraspol.
7 Jun — Chisinau: Government activates energy contingency plan; Russian gas supply interruptions continue.
countries.coa: Moldova faces moderate risk from Russian hybrid pressure and Transnistria destabilisation. EU accession track provides political insulation. No kinetic escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Georgia
incidents: 13 Jun — Tbilisi: Anti-EU demonstrations suppressed; 22 activists detained; Georgian Dream accelerates Russia-aligned legislation.
10 Jun — S. Ossetia: Russian garrison conducts unannounced patrol exercise on administrative boundary line; Georgia lodges protest.
8 Jun — Abkhazia: Occupied territory continues to deny EU humanitarian mission access.
countries.coa: Georgia faces dual threat: Russian military presence in occupied territories and internal political crisis as Georgian Dream shifts toward Moscow. EU and NATO integration at serious risk. Domestic civil resistance movement remains active but suppressed.

countries.name: Armenia
incidents: 13 Jun — Yerevan: PM Pashinyan reaffirms EU integration pathway; Russia responds by suspending arms delivery contracts.
10 Jun — Armenia-Azerbaijan border: Minor firing incident; 1 soldier wounded; EU monitors present.
8 Jun — Yerevan: Armenia formally suspends participation in additional CSTO joint exercises; withdrawal discussions deepen.
countries.coa: Armenia is pivoting toward the EU and West following the 2023 Nagorno-Karabakh defeat. Risk of renewed Azerbaijani military action remains moderate. Russia-Armenia relations will continue deteriorating — CSTO formal withdrawal likely within 12 months.

countries.name: Azerbaijan
incidents: 12 Jun — Baku: Aliyev renews Zangezur corridor demands; signals willingness for economic pressure on Armenia.
9 Jun — Karabakh: Azerbaijani forces consolidate control; ethnic Armenian outmigration continues.
7 Jun — AZ-Iran Border: Military patrolling increased amid US-Iran exchange escalation; no incidents.
countries.coa: Azerbaijan leverages its strategic position confidently. Zangezur corridor access remains the primary diplomatic focus. AZ-Iran relations remain tense amid broader regional escalation. No major military action expected within 90 days.

regions.name: Sahel
overview: Nigeria breakthrough: Army frees 360 Boko Haram abductees in Borno (7 Jun); joint Nigeria-US precision strike kills 21 ISWAP fighters including 3 commanders (10 Jun); ISWAP leader killed in Borno (12 Jun) — most significant operational week for Nigeria's northeast in 2026.

JNIM Bamako siege ongoing: April 25 JNIM/FLA offensive continues to strangle Bamako supply lines; Defense Minister Camara killed; JNIM holds territory from Kidal to Mopti; Bamako encirclement risk remains HIGH.
Burkina Faso: JNIM cordon around Dori tightening; MSF suspended operations in 3 districts; internet blackouts and journalist detentions signal growing regime fragility.
Sudan: RSF maintains strategic control of Darfur; commander defections signal internal RSF fractures; Kordofan is the active kinetic front; 270,000+ killed and 24.6 million facing acute hunger.

countries.name: Mali
incidents: 12 Jun — Mopti Region: JNIM siege operations continue; FAMa supply convoy ambushed near Djenné; 4 soldiers killed.
11 Jun — Bamako: Junta extends fuel rationing into central Bamako districts as JNIM supply-line strangulation tightens.
9 Jun — Kidal: JNIM/FLA maintain combined hold on northern territories; Wagner advisors restrict external access to region.
countries.coa: JNIM's Bamako siege — declared after the catastrophic April 25 offensive that killed Defense Minister Camara — is steadily tightening. FAMa cannot dislodge JNIM without Wagner reinforcement, which is stretched thin. Junta legitimacy declining rapidly. Bamako encirclement risk within 90 days remains HIGH if current trajectory holds.

countries.name: Niger
incidents: 12 Jun — Tillabéri: ISWAP-linked attack on gendarmerie post; 5 killed.
10 Jun — Diffa: Cross-border incursion; 2 villages raided, 300+ displaced.
8 Jun — Niamey: CNSP announces deepened bilateral security agreement with Russia; Western counter-terrorism partnerships formally dissolved.
countries.coa: ISWAP and JNIM cross-border coordination in Tillabéri and Diffa zones will intensify. CNSP is consolidating Russia-Iran security arrangements. With Western counter-terrorism presence fully expelled, no external pressure exists to constrain militant expansion.

countries.name: Burkina Faso
incidents: 13 Jun — Dori (Sahel Region): JNIM cordon tightens further; aid convoy turned back; VDP militia losses continue.
11 Jun — Ouagadougou: Internet blackout imposed for 14 hours; 6 journalists detained in ongoing information suppression.
9 Jun — Est Region: MSF suspends operations in third district following direct threats from armed groups.
7 Jun — Boucle du Mouhoun: Village attack displaces 2,200; IDP camp at 140% capacity.
countries.coa: Burkina Faso has the most severe humanitarian trajectory in the region. JNIM encirclement of Dori is tightening — siege conditions possible within 30 days. Information blackouts signal growing regime fragility. Junta survival depends on Russian-Wagner support, which is already committed to Mali.

countries.name: Sudan
incidents: 13 Jun — N. Kordofan: RSF artillery strike on IDP settlement; 14 killed, 30+ wounded.
11 Jun — Darfur: RSF maintains total blockade on humanitarian access to El Fasher; UN confirms 6,000 RSF killings since October 2025.
9 Jun — Khartoum/Omdurman: SAF airstrikes on RSF positions; civilian infrastructure damaged.
8 Jun — Port Sudan: RSF long-range drone reaches Port Sudan administrative capital outskirts; strategic reach demonstrated.
countries.coa: Sudan's is the world's largest humanitarian crisis — 270,000+ killed, 24.6 million facing acute hunger. El Fasher fell to RSF in October 2025; UN confirms genocide conditions in Darfur. RSF commander defections signal internal fractures but not collapse. No ceasefire pathway visible; international pressure has failed to alter RSF behaviour.

countries.name: Chad
incidents: 12 Jun — Lake Chad: ISWAP attack on fishing settlement; 5 killed.
10 Jun — Tibesti: ANT patrol clashes with armed group; 2 soldiers wounded.
8 Jun — N'Djamena: Transitional council delays election roadmap for fifth time; democratic transition indefinitely deferred.
countries.coa: Chad maintains relative stability as regional anchor. ISWAP Lake Chad operations will not significantly escalate without further Borno-side enablement. Mahamat Déby will continue delaying elections — no viable democratic transition within 90 days.

countries.name: Mauritania
incidents: 12 Jun — Hodh Ech Chargui: Security forces intercept JNIM-linked weapons cache; 4 arrested.
10 Jun — Néma: Precautionary village evacuation near Mali border due to JNIM spillover threat.
8 Jun — Nouakchott: President Ghazouani holds bilateral security meeting with Senegalese counterpart.
countries.coa: Mauritania remains the most stable Sahel state. Primary risk is Mali spillover into border zone. No kinetic escalation expected within 90 days — strong security services and community engagement provide resilience.

countries.name: Nigeria
incidents: 12 Jun — Borno State: Nigerian soldiers kill senior ISWAP leader in Borno; separately foil attempted kidnap of WAEC examination students in southeast Nigeria.
10 Jun — Borno (Arege): Joint Nigeria-US intelligence-led precision strike kills 21 ISWAP fighters including 3 mid-level commanders; weapons factory destroyed.
7 Jun — Borno: Nigerian army frees 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in rescue operation in southern Borno.
9 Jun — Zamfara: Bandit group abducts 12 farmers in separate Sahel-region attack; ransom demanded.
countries.coa: Nigeria achieved significant operational success in Borno this week — 360 abductees freed and ISWAP leader killed in addition to the joint Nigeria-US strike. However, banditry in the northwest and secessionist pressure in the southeast remain unresolved. ISWAP expansion toward Adamawa is the primary 90-day risk.

regions.name: Asia-Pacific
overview: Post-Xi-Kim summit dynamics: DPRK seeking economic guarantees following Xi Jinping's June 8–9 state visit to Pyongyang; China-DPRK strategic partnership deepening complicates US-ROK-Japan deterrence calculus; DPRK fired 7th ballistic missile of 2026 hours before Xi's arrival.

Myanmar resistance alliance: New unified resistance command formed April 2026 is the most organised challenge to junta authority since the 2021 coup; junta controls fewer than 40% of townships; resistance holds 42%.

South China Sea: PLA exercises east of Luzon in response to Balikatan 2026; CCG recorded 7th Kinmen incursion of 2026; US-Philippines-Australia joint patrol conducted; grey-zone normalisation accelerating.
India-Pakistan ceasefire: May 2025 ceasefire holding at 13 months; LoC tensions managed; 2026 Bajaur attack raises KPK threat; BLA/TTP activity ongoing in Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

countries.name: North Korea
incidents: 9 Jun — Pyongyang: Xi Jinping concludes 2-day state visit; China-DPRK strategic partnership and economic cooperation reaffirmed.
8 Jun — East Sea: DPRK fires 7th ballistic missile of 2026 (IRBM-class) hours before Xi's arrival as leverage demonstration.
13 Jun — DMZ: North Korean troops expand fortification works on southern boundary; additional military road construction observed by satellite.
countries.coa: Xi visit designed to give DPRK economic relief while maintaining strategic ambiguity on denuclearisation. China-DPRK partnership deepening complicates US-ROK-Japan deterrence calculus. Additional missile tests expected as Kim converts Xi visit leverage into further demands. Denuclearisation probability in 90 days: zero.

countries.name: Myanmar
incidents: 13 Jun — Shan State: Arakan Army advances toward Lashio; junta loses village cluster after air and artillery engagement.
11 Jun — Rakhine State: Junta airstrike on Arakan Army position; 7 civilians killed; displacement accelerating.
9 Jun — Sagaing: PDF ambush on junta supply column; 5 soldiers killed.
8 Jun — Naypyidaw: Junta announces further expanded conscription; battlefield losses across Shan, Rakhine, and Sagaing continue.
countries.coa: Junta now controls fewer than 40% of Myanmar's townships; resistance holds 42%. New unified resistance alliance formed April 2026 is the most significant command development since 2021 coup. Junta survival strategy relies on air power — over 3,300 strikes in 2025-2026. Territorial collapse in key provinces possible within 6–12 months if resistance maintains command unity.

countries.name: Pakistan
incidents: 12 Jun — Bajaur (KPK): Major militant attack in Bajaur district; security forces engaged in extended firefight; casualties reported.
10 Jun — Balochistan: BLA attack on gas pipeline; energy infrastructure disrupted.
8 Jun — KPK: TTP ambush on Frontier Corps patrol near Peshawar; 3 soldiers killed.
countries.coa: Pakistan faces simultaneous internal pressures: TTP in KPK including the significant Bajaur attack, BLA in Balochistan, and Afghan border tensions. India-Pakistan ceasefire holding at 13 months but structurally fragile. Economic deterioration compounds security challenges.

countries.name: India
incidents: 13 Jun — Kashmir (LoC): BSF reports continued cross-border firing; ceasefire technically intact.
11 Jun — Arunachal Pradesh: PLA troops reportedly cross LAC briefly; India lodges diplomatic protest.
9 Jun — Manipur: Ethnic violence continues in Churachandpur; security forces deployed.
countries.coa: India maintains elevated military posture vs. both Pakistan and China. Manipur ethnic conflict drains domestic security resources. LAC tensions are a persistent secondary concern. India-Pakistan ceasefire remains structurally fragile despite 13-month hold.

countries.name: China
incidents: 13 Jun — East China Sea/Luzon: PLA conducts transit exercise with 12 vessels east of Luzon; Taiwan and Philippines scramble assets.
11 Jun — Kinmen Islands: CCG conducts 7th incursion into restricted Kinmen waters in 2026; Taiwan coast guard responds.
9 Jun — Pyongyang: Xi Jinping concludes North Korea state visit; China-DPRK strategic partnership deepened.
countries.coa: China will maintain grey-zone pressure on Taiwan and the Philippines while avoiding direct kinetic action. CCG Kinmen and Pratas incursions are a normalisation strategy. PLA exercises east of Luzon counter US-Philippines military cooperation. No major military action vs. Taiwan expected within 90 days but grey-zone intensity will increase.

countries.name: Taiwan
incidents: 13 Jun — Taiwan Strait: PLA conducts transit exercise with 12 vessels; Taiwan scrambles fighter aircraft and coast guard assets.
11 Jun — Kinmen Islands: CCG 7th intrusion into restricted Kinmen waters in 2026; Taiwan coast guard conducts intercept.
9 Jun — Taipei: Taiwan defence ministry condemns Xi-Kim summit outcome; calls for UNSC response and trilateral US-Japan-ROK solidarity.
countries.coa: Taiwan faces sustained grey-zone pressure designed to normalise PLA presence around outlying islands and in the ADIZ. CCG incursion pattern at Kinmen and Pratas is a systematic desensitisation strategy. Cross-strait kinetic conflict risk is LOW in 90 days but grey-zone intensity will increase through remainder of 2026.

countries.name: Philippines
incidents: 13 Jun — South China Sea: Philippines coast guard confronts CCG vessel at Scarborough Shoal; water cannon deployed.
11 Jun — Luzon: Philippines protests PLA exercises east of Luzon; US reaffirms alliance commitment.
8 Jun — Mindanao: Abu Sayyaf kidnap-for-ransom operation; 2 hostages taken in Sulu.
countries.coa: Philippines faces persistent Chinese grey-zone pressure in South China Sea. US-Philippines alliance plus France SOFA strengthens Manila's deterrent posture. Mindanao internal security is manageable. Risk of miscalculation at Scarborough Shoal remains elevated.

countries.name: South Korea
incidents: 13 Jun — Seoul: ROK NSC convenes over DPRK DMZ fortification expansion and post-Xi-Kim summit dynamics.
11 Jun — Yellow Sea: US-ROK joint naval exercise continues; deterrence posture reinforced.
9 Jun — Seoul: ROK condemns China-DPRK partnership deepening; requests emergency trilateral US-Japan-ROK consultations.
countries.coa: South Korea will intensify US-ROK combined deterrence following Xi-Kim summit. China-DPRK strategic partnership deepening is the primary strategic concern — it complicates the pressure pathway on DPRK. ROK will accelerate trilateral cooperation with US and Japan.

countries.name: Vietnam
incidents: 12 Jun — South China Sea: Vietnamese fishing vessels harassed by Chinese coast guard; 1 boat damaged.
10 Jun — Hanoi: Vietnam lodges diplomatic protest over Chinese CCG activity at Paracel Islands.
8 Jun — Ho Chi Minh City: US-Vietnam defence cooperation meeting advances framework agreement.
countries.coa: Vietnam faces persistent grey-zone friction with China. 'Bamboo diplomacy' maintains relations with all major powers. US-Vietnam defence cooperation quietly deepening. No kinetic escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Japan
incidents: 13 Jun — Tokyo: Japan requests trilateral emergency consultations with US and ROK over post-Xi-Kim summit developments.
11 Jun — East China Sea: Chinese survey vessel operates in Japan's EEZ; Japan coast guard maintains surveillance.
countries.coa: Japan faces a deteriorating strategic environment — China-DPRK summit adds complexity to Northeast Asian deterrence — but no imminent kinetic threat. Counter-strike capability programme accelerating. Japan-US alliance remains the primary deterrent.

countries.name: Indonesia
incidents: 12 Jun — Natuna Sea: Indonesia navy deploys additional vessels following Chinese fishing fleet incursion into EEZ.
9 Jun — Papua: OPM separatist group fires on military post; no casualties reported.
countries.coa: Indonesia maintaining non-aligned posture while quietly strengthening maritime security. Natuna Sea incursions are a persistent Chinese grey-zone tactic. Papua OPM remains a low-level internal concern.

countries.name: Thailand
incidents: 12 Jun — Deep South: Separatist IED detonates on patrol route; 1 soldier lightly injured.
9 Jun — Bangkok: Government confirms third round of renewed dialogue with MARA Patani resuming.
countries.coa: Thailand's Deep South separatist conflict remains a contained low-level challenge. MARA Patani dialogue showing limited progress. Bangkok's political stability has improved.

countries.name: Bangladesh
incidents: 12 Jun — Dhaka: Interim government extends crackdown on Islamist networks; 10 JMB suspects arrested.
9 Jun — Cox's Bazar: Rohingya camp inter-gang violence; 3 killed, UN access temporarily restricted.
countries.coa: Bangladesh faces internal governance challenges post-2024 transition. Rohingya crisis in Cox's Bazar remains a persistent source of instability. External threat level is LOW.

countries.name: Sri Lanka
incidents: 11 Jun — Colombo: IMF third review completed positively; economic reform programme on track.
9 Jun — Northern Province: Tamil political tensions over land restitution; demonstrations remain peaceful.
countries.coa: Sri Lanka is in economic recovery mode post-2022 crisis. Security environment is stable — no active conflict. Chinese debt diplomacy remains a structural strategic competition concern.

regions.name: Americas
overview: Cuba crisis escalating: Trump administration sanctions Cuba's national oil company Cupet (11 Jun); UN experts condemn USS Nimitz Caribbean deployment as unlawful coercion; student protests and energy blackouts deepening — Cuba faces most severe crisis since the 1990s Special Period.

Colombia: Presidential runoff 21 Jun — far-right de la Espriella (44%) vs. left-wing Cepeda (41%); ELN maintaining unilateral ceasefire until vote; EMC/EMBF active in Nariño and Cauca regardless of election outcome.

Venezuela: Post-Maduro transitional authority consolidating with US support; Maduro loyalist guerrilla networks targeting oil infrastructure; joint Venezuela-Colombia operations ongoing in Apure border zone.

Haiti: GSF (successor to Kenya-led MSS since March 2026) operating with under-strength force; gangs control 90%+ of Port-au-Prince; Chad replacement troops still in US training — 5,500-strong mandate nowhere near met.

countries.name: Venezuela
incidents: 13 Jun — Maracaibo: Oil infrastructure restoration continues; loyalist sabotage network disrupted by transitional authority security operation.
11 Jun — Valencia: US-supported transitional government operation degrades Maduro loyalist network; 6 suspects arrested.
9 Jun — Colombia Border: Joint Colombia-Venezuela operation continues against irregular Venezuelan armed group in Apure region.
7 Jun — Caracas: Transitional authority disrupts Maduro loyalist cell; weapons cache and communications equipment seized.
countries.coa: Venezuela's protracted post-intervention stabilisation continues. Maduro loyalist networks will maintain asymmetric resistance targeting oil infrastructure. International recognition remains divided. Transitional authority is consolidating but lacks full territorial control. Cuban security personnel withdrawal has added further instability.

countries.name: Haiti
incidents: 13 Jun — Port-au-Prince: GSF operating with approximately 1,400 personnel against 5,500-strong mandate; gangs control 90%+ of capital.
11 Jun — Cité Soleil: Viv Ansanm coalition attacks GSF patrol; 1 officer wounded, 5 gang members killed.
10 Jun — Artibonite: Gang expansion into agricultural zone continues; food supply disrupted, 1,200 displaced.
8 Jun — Port-au-Prince: HNP/GSF joint operation frees 18 kidnap victims in Tabarre.
countries.coa: GSF (successor to Kenya-led MSS since March 2026) lacks the 5,500 mandated personnel to achieve meaningful gang degradation — Chad replacement troops are still training in the US. Gang territorial control is expanding; risk of additional urban centres falling within 90 days is HIGH. Force generation must accelerate or Haiti will remain ungovernable.

countries.name: Colombia
incidents: 13 Jun — Nariño: EMBF attack on Colombian military patrol; 3 soldiers wounded.
11 Jun — Cauca: EMC ambush on police patrol; 2 officers wounded.
9 Jun — Arauca: ELN ceasefire violations continue in Arauca despite formal ceasefire declared until June 21 runoff.
8 Jun — Venezuela Border: Joint Colombia-Venezuela operation disrupts armed group in Apure zone.
countries.coa: Colombia's June 21 presidential runoff is the defining event of the next 90 days. De la Espriella (far-right, 44%) vs. Cepeda (left-wing, 41%) — outcome will determine the approach to the peace process and US relations. ELN ceasefire is a tactical pause for the vote; expect full resumption post-election. EMC and EMBF will continue regardless of electoral outcome.

countries.name: Cuba
incidents: 13 Jun — New York/Havana: UN experts formally condemn USS Nimitz Caribbean deployment as 'unlawful coercion'; Havana calls for international solidarity.
11 Jun — Havana: Trump administration sanctions Cuba's national oil company Cupet; deepens island-wide energy crisis.
11 Jun — Havana University: Students stage sit-in over energy crisis-driven class cancellations; riot police called.
9 Jun — Santiago de Cuba: Power outages trigger spontaneous demonstrations; government deploys rapid-response units.
countries.coa: Cuba is facing its deepest humanitarian and energy crisis since the 1990s Special Period. US sanctions on Cupet (June 11) compound the loss of Venezuelan oil supply and the February 2026 US blockade on oil tankers. The protest movement is gaining cross-class composition. Government will suppress militarily but faces growing capacity constraints. Risk of regime-threatening upheaval within 6–12 months is elevated.

countries.name: Ecuador
incidents: 13 Jun — Esmeraldas: Cartel attack on coastal town; 4 killed including 2 police.
11 Jun — Guayaquil: Prison riot linked to Los Lobos/R7 gang conflict; 2 inmates killed.
9 Jun — Quito: Government reviews state of exception in 5 provinces; extension likely.
countries.coa: Ecuador faces entrenched cartel violence (Los Lobos, R7, La Firma) that has outpaced state capacity. State of exception provides temporary relief but no structural improvement expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Peru
incidents: 12 Jun — VRAEM: SL-VRAEM attack on coca eradication team; 1 police officer killed.
10 Jun — Lima: Anti-government protest over economy and political instability; 8,000 demonstrators.
8 Jun — Loreto: 2 killed in illegal mining territorial dispute.
countries.coa: Peru's challenges remain primarily internal: SL-VRAEM insurgency, illegal mining violence, and political instability. President Boluarte government is fragile but functional. No external threat.

countries.name: Bolivia
incidents: 12 Jun — Santa Cruz: Protests over court ruling continue; transport disruptions persist.
10 Jun — La Paz: MAS faction conflict between Arce and Morales camps intensifies.
8 Jun — Chapare: Cocalero mobilisation over development programme funding cuts.
countries.coa: Bolivia faces political instability rather than security crisis. MAS internal factional conflict creates governance uncertainty. No armed conflict at scale expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Dominican Republic
incidents: 12 Jun — Santo Domingo: Government deports 1,200 irregular Haitian migrants; border closures extended.
10 Jun — Dajabón: Border crossing closed due to gang proximity in Haiti; trade disrupted.
countries.coa: Dominican Republic faces Haiti spillover risks but maintains internal security stability. Primary monitoring requirement: Haitian gang advance toward Dominican border zone.

countries.name: Jamaica
incidents: 12 Jun — Kingston: West Kingston state of public emergency extended; gang violence continues.
10 Jun — Montego Bay: Tourism zone security operation; 1 gang member killed, 7 arrested.
countries.coa: Jamaica faces persistent gang violence in Kingston and tourism zone security challenges. No geopolitical or state-level threat; organised crime is the primary concern.
